specification.topic-statement: The Google content block serializer transforms canonical message content blocks into the parts format required by the Gemini API, including role mapping per model series and per-type part construction for text, thinking, tool call, tool result, and redacted thinking blocks.

scope.in-scope: Role string mapping for user, assistant, and tool roles, content block serialization for all canonical block types (text, thinking, tool call, tool result, redacted thinking), tool result name lookup by backward scan, thought signature injection as a leading part for the first assistant message, and thought signature retrieval from assistant message metadata.
scope.boundary: How the parts produced here are assembled into the full Gemini request JSON body (systemInstruction, contents array, tools, toolConfig, generationConfig) is covered by the Google request serialization specification.
How thought signatures are extracted from streaming responses and stored in message metadata is covered by the Google streaming event processing specification.
How abstract thinking levels are translated into numeric budgets or level strings is covered by the Google thinking configuration specification.

field.name: tool role
field.note: maps to 'user' for Gemini 3 models; maps to 'function' for all other models

field.name: text
field.note: the text content of the block

field.name: thought
field.note: always true; marks the part as a thinking part

field.name: functionCall
field.note: nested object containing name and args

field.name: functionCall.args
field.note: the tool arguments as a parsed JSON object, not a string

field.name: functionResponse
field.note: nested object containing name and response

field.name: functionResponse.name
field.note: the function name looked up from prior messages, or the tool call ID as fallback

field.name: thoughtSignature
field.note: the opaque thought signature string; injected as a standalone part object

behavior.role-mapping:
Each message has a canonical role that is mapped to a Gemini role string before serialization. The mapping is:
- User role → "user" - Assistant role → "model" - Tool role → "user" when the model is Gemini 3; "function" for all other models (including Gemini 2.5, other Gemini models, and null model)
Role determination for the tool role performs a model series check. When the model identifier is null, the tool role maps to "function" (the non-Gemini-3 path).
Gemini 3 introduced a requirement that tool result messages use the "user" role rather than the older "function" role. Sending "function" role to a Gemini 3 endpoint is incorrect. The serializer branches on model series to emit the appropriate role string.
behavior.parts-assembly:
Each message's content is serialized into a "parts" array. The parts array is constructed in two phases:
Phase 1 — Thought signature injection: If a thought signature is available and the current message is the first assistant-role message encountered during iteration, a standalone thought signature part is prepended as the first element of the parts array. This part is an object with a single "thoughtSignature" key set to the signature string.
Phase 2 — Content block parts: Each content block in the message is serialized in order and appended to the parts array.
The thought signature is injected at most once per request, into the first assistant message in the conversation. Subsequent assistant messages receive no injected signature part, even if a signature is available.
The thought signature value passed during serialization is null for non-Gemini-3 models. Phase 1 is a no-op when the signature is null, so no thoughtSignature part is ever injected for non-Gemini-3 models.
behavior.text-block-serialization:
A text content block produces a single part object with one field: - "text": the text string from the block
No additional fields are added.
behavior.thinking-block-serialization:
A thinking content block produces a single part object with two fields: - "text": the thinking text from the block - "thought": true (boolean)
The "thought" field distinguishes this part from a regular text part when the Gemini API returns or receives it. Both fields are always present together.
behavior.redacted-thinking-block-serialization:
A redacted thinking content block produces no output. The block is silently skipped and no part object is added to the parts array.
Unlike Anthropic serialization which re-emits redacted thinking blocks with their opaque data, the Google serializer discards them. Gemini does not have an equivalent concept for redacted thinking.
behavior.tool-call-block-serialization:
A tool call content block produces a single part object with one field: - "functionCall": an object containing "name" and "args"
The "name" field is the tool name string. The "args" field is the tool arguments parsed from their stored JSON string representation into a live JSON object. The arguments are embedded as a JSON object, not a string.
Tool call arguments in canonical form are stored as a JSON string. During serialization the string is parsed into a JSON object before embedding. The Gemini API requires args as a structured object, unlike the OpenAI Chat Completions API which accepts a string.
behavior.tool-result-block-serialization:
A tool result content block produces a single part object with one field: - "functionResponse": an object containing "name" and "response"
The "name" field is resolved by scanning prior messages (see tool result name lookup). The "response" field is an object with a single "content" key set to the result content string.
The result content string is not placed directly under "functionResponse". It is nested one level deeper inside a "response" object with the key "content". This nesting is required by the Gemini API format.
behavior.tool-result-name-lookup:
A tool result block carries a tool call ID that refers to a prior tool call. To serialize the result, the corresponding tool name must be found. The name is not stored directly in the tool result block; it must be retrieved by scanning the message history.
The lookup scans all messages before the current message index, in forward order (oldest first). For each message, all content blocks are checked. When a tool call block is found whose ID matches the tool result's tool call ID, the tool name from that block is returned immediately.
If no matching tool call block is found after scanning all prior messages, the tool call ID itself is used as the function name.
If the matching tool call is not found in prior messages — for example because it was truncated from the context window — the tool call ID string is used verbatim as the function name in the serialized functionResponse. This produces a syntactically valid but semantically incorrect response; the Gemini API may reject it or treat it as an unknown function.
The scan covers only messages at indices strictly less than the current message index. The current message and any later messages are not examined. This ensures only causally prior tool calls are candidates.
behavior.thought-signature-retrieval:
Before request serialization begins, the most recent thought signature is retrieved from the message history for Gemini 3 models.
The retrieval scans messages in reverse order (newest first). Only assistant-role messages are examined. For each candidate assistant message, the message metadata field is inspected. If the metadata is non-null, non-empty, and parseable as JSON, the "thought_signature" key is extracted. If the key is present, is a string, and is non-empty, its value is returned as the thought signature.
Scanning stops at the first assistant message that yields a valid signature. If no such message is found, the result is null.
The retrieved signature is copied into owned storage before the parsed metadata document is released, ensuring the signature string remains valid for the duration of serialization.
User messages, tool messages, and system messages are skipped entirely during the reverse scan. Only messages with the assistant role can carry a thought signature in their metadata.
If an assistant message's metadata field contains malformed JSON or a JSON structure that does not have "thought_signature" as a non-empty string, that message is skipped and the scan continues to the next older assistant message. No error is reported.
The entire reverse scan is short-circuited when the model is not classified as Gemini 3. The returned signature is null unconditionally for non-Gemini-3 models, even if thought signatures happen to be stored in the message metadata.
